scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for sshd_enable_strictmodes (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sshd_enable_strictmodes). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message:
{
  "approved": true,
  "review_approved": true,
  "review_feedback": "The plan would enable StrictModes by setting it in a compliance file, but the specific file path referenced in the remediation script (/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf) may not be standard or guaranteed to be read by sshd on all systems. While the approach is functionally correct if the file is included, the file path choice introduces uncertainty.",
  "review_concerns": [
    "The file path /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf is non-standard and may not be included in the SSH configuration if the system lacks the appropriate Include directive in sshd_config."
  ],
  "review_suggested_improvements": [
    "Use a standard configuration file path such as /etc/ssh/sshd_config or a conventionally named drop-in file in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/ (e.g., 00-ssh-settings.conf) to ensure the setting is definitively applied."
  ],
  "review_security_score": 10,
  "qa_safe": true,
  "qa_verdict_reason": "Creating a config file to set StrictModes yes is a harmless change; if the file is not included by sshd, it has no effect, and if included, it only enforces existing secure behavior without disrupting service.",
  "qa_side_effects": [],
  "qa_recommendation": "Approve",
  "instruction": "Plan approved. Proceed with run_cmd / read_file / write_file to apply the fix."
}

- Write content "StrictModes yes" into that file (write_file maybe overwriting)
- Set permissions 0600 (chmod) via run_cmd? Actually we have only run_cmd, read_file, write_file. We can use run_cmd to execute chmod.

Thus plan:

- run_cmd: mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d
- run_cmd: touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf
- write_file: path = /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf, content = "StrictModes yes", mode = "0600"? Actually write_file likely can set mode? The description: Write content to a single file on the target host. It may have mode param. We'll set mode "0600".
- Then run_cmd: chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf (if write_file didn't set mode). But we can set mode in write_file.
